zh.lifeSummary: 當John Bernard於1394年出生在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆時，他的父親Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton29歲，母親Elizabeth Lilling24歲。他於1410年與Ellen Mallory結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1452年3月24日在英國英格蘭素福爾可阿肯哈姆享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Bernard于1394年出生在英国英格兰坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆时，他的父亲Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton29岁，母亲Elizabeth Lilling24岁。他于1410年与Ellen Mallory结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1452年3月24日在英国英格兰素福爾可阿肯哈姆享年58岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Bernard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Andre, Lucien, Normand, Henri, Michel, Cecile, Gabrielle.

English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, German, Polish, Czech, Slovenian, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Tanzania): from the personal name Bernard, from an ancient Germanic name, composed of the elements ber(n) ‘bear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’ (see Bernhard ). It was borne by several Christian saints, including Saint Bernard of Menthon (923–1008), founder of Alpine hospices and patron saint of mountaineers, whose cult accounts for the frequency of the name in Alpine regions. The popularity of the personal name was also greatly increased by virtue of its having been borne by Saint Bernard of Clairvaux (c. 1090–1153), founder and abbot of the Cistercian monastery at Clairvaux. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Bernardo . Bernard is the second most frequent surname in France. Compare Barnard , Benore , and Burnor .

History: The first documented bearer of this name in Canada was from the Lorraine region of France. He is recorded in Quebec City, QC, in 1666 as Jean Bernard. He and some of his descendants were dit Anse or Hanse, presumably because his original forename was Hans (German equivalent of French Jean). — The Bernard families of LA have origins in France, Acadia, the West Indies, and Germany. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Bernhard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alfons, Detlef, Dieter, Erwin, Friedhelm, Fritz, Gerhard, Hertha, Horst, Kurt, Manfred, Otto.

German, Dutch, Danish, Swedish, Norwegian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the ancient Germanic personal name Bernhard, composed of the elements ber(n) ‘bear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’. As a Jewish name, this is an adoption of the German name because it has the same beginning as the Jewish name Ber . The surname of German origin is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). See also Bernard , compare Barnard , Barnhard , Barnhart , and Bernhardt .

variants.name: Barnas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Bronislawa, Jerzy, Leszek, Pawel, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zbigniew.
Polish (Barnaś); Slovak (Barnáš and Barnaš): from a derivative of (local forms of) the personal name Bernard .
Dutch: unexplained.

variants.name: Barnett
meanings: English: habitational name from various places, for example Chipping (High) Barnet, East Barnet, and Friern Barnet in Greater London, named with Old English bærnet ‘place cleared by burning’ (a derivative of bærnan ‘to burn, to set light to’).
English (of Norman origin): from a medieval personal name, a variant of   Bernard  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): this surname has been adopted by Ashkenazic Jews in the English-speaking world, perhaps as an Anglicized form of a vaguely similar Jewish name such as   Baruch  .

variants.name: Barnard
meanings: English, Dutch, and French: variant of Bernard and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Barnard is very rare in France.
Americanized form of German   Bernhard   or   Bernhardt  , and of German, Polish, Czech, and Slovenian   Bernard  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. John Barnard was one of the founders of Hartford, CT, in 1635 (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker). Another John Barnard, born in Boston in 1681, was a Congregational clergyman who served as minister of Marblehead, MA, from 1716 to 1770.

variants.name: Barney
meanings: English: habitational name from Barney in Norfolk, which is probably named with an Old English personal name Bera (with genitive -n) + Old English ēg ‘island, dry ground in a marsh’.
English: from the personal name Barney, a pet form of Bernard .
Scottish: possibly from the Old Norse personal name Bjarni (from bjǫrn ‘bear’).

variants.name: Berka
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Berka, named with ancient Germanic birka ‘birch tree’, notably in Hesse and Thuringia.
Czech, Slovak, and Polish: from a pet form of the personal name   Bernard  .
Czech: nickname from berka ‘robber’.

variants.name: Benore
meanings: Americanized form of French Bernard , reflecting the characteristic American French (and Canadian) rounding of the -ard ending.

variants.name: Benard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Alain, Armand, Emile, Francois, Henri, Jacques, Patrice, Pierre, Serge.
French (Bénard): variant of Bernard .
History: René Bénard/Besnard dit Bourjoli from Villiers-au-Bouin in Indre-et-Loire, France, married Marie Sédilot in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1661.

variants.name: Barnick
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Kurt.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Barnik: from a pet form of the personal names beginning with Barn-, based on the Pomeranian derivative of the Slavic name element bran ‘to defend’ (from Old Slavic branъ ‘defence’). Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Bernard or a cognate of the Jewish name below.

Germanized or Americanized form of Sorbian Barnik: nickname from a shortened form of Baranik, a diminutive of Baran ‘ram’, or perhaps an occupational name from a shortened form of Old Polish bartnik ‘bee keeper’ (compare Bartnik ).

variants.name: Bera
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Madan, Dilip, Girish, Kanta, Mansukh, Raj, Rajesh, Rajiv, Ranjan, Sonali, Tapan.  Spanish Ruben, Ana, Balentina, Gilberto, Gonzalo, Juana.
Indian (West Bengal) and Bangladeshi: Kayastha name of unexplained etymology.
Catalan (Berà): habitational name from a place called Berà in Tarragona, probably named with the medieval personal name Bera(ne) of Visigothic origin.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When John Bernard was born in 1394, in Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton, was 29 and his mother, Elizabeth Lilling, was 24. He married Ellen Mallory in 1410. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 24 March 1452, in Akenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Bernard est né en 1394, à Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Lilling, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Ellen Mallory en 1410. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 24 mars 1452, à Akenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Bernard 1394 in Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton 29 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Lilling 24. Er hat 1410 Ellen Mallory geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 24. März 1452 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Akenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Bernard è nato il nel 1394 nel Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Lilling, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Ellen Mallory nel 1410. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 24 marzo 1452, nel Akenham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Bernardが1394年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアイスレハムで生まれた時，父Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northamptonは29歳，母Elizabeth Lillingは24歳であった。1410年にEllen Malloryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1452年3月24日にイギリスイギリススフォルクアケンハムで58歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Bernard(이)가 1394년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 이슬레함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Lilling(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1410년 Ellen Mallory(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1452년 3월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 악함에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Bernard nasceu em 1394, em Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Lilling, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Ellen Mallory em 1410. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de março de 1452, em Akenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Bernard родился в 1394 г. в местности ислэам, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Lilling, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen Mallory в 1410 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 24 марта 1452 г. в местности акенам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Bernard nació en 1394, en Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Bernard Sheriff of Northampton, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Lilling, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Ellen Mallory en 1410. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1452, en Akenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
